---
title: "Survivorship"
author: "Jill Ashey"
date: "2026-05-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

Read in survivorship data
```{r}
surv <- read.csv("../data/Physiology/survivorship_experiment.csv", header = T)
```

Read in sample metadata and join with surv df by sample ID
```{r}
meta <- read.csv("../data/sample_manifest.csv", header = T)

surv_meta <- surv %>%
  left_join(meta, by = "SampleID")
```

```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(survival)
library(survminer)

# 1. Clean up and calculate experimental days
surv_cleaned <- surv_meta %>%
  mutate(
    # Convert text dates to actual Date objects
    Date = as.Date(Date, format = "%m/%d/%y"),
    
    # Calculate Day of Experiment assuming 5/10/26 is Day 0
    Start_Date = as.Date("2026-05-10"),
    Days = as.numeric(Date - Start_Date),
    
    # Create a proper Event binary column (1 = Dead, 0 = Censored/Alive)
    # Your Censored column is already 0 for alive. We make sure Dead = 1.
    Event = ifelse(Status == "Dead", 1, 0)
  )

# 2. Filter out rows AFTER a coral was destructively sampled
# If they were sampled (Censored = 1), they should not contribute data past that day.
# (Note: Standard survival objects handle right-censoring automatically if formatted correctly)

# Create the survival object: Surv(time, event)
surv_object <- Surv(time = surv_cleaned$Days, event = surv_cleaned$Event)

# Fit a curve comparing Treatment and Phenotype
fit_treatment <- survfit(surv_object ~ Treatment, data = surv_cleaned)
fit_phenotype <- survfit(surv_object ~ Phenotype, data = surv_cleaned)

# Fit a curve looking at the interaction of both
fit_both <- survfit(surv_object ~ Treatment + Phenotype, data = surv_cleaned)

ggsurvplot(
  fit_both,
  data = surv_cleaned,
  pval = TRUE,             # Adds p-value from a log-rank test
  conf.int = TRUE,         # Adds confidence intervals
  risk.table = TRUE,       # Adds a text table showing number of corals remaining at risk
  #legend.labs = c("Amb:Res", "Amb:Sus", "High:Res", "High:Sus"), # Adjust order based on your levels
  palette = "Dark2",       # Color palette
  xlab = "Days of Experiment",
  ylab = "Survival Probability",
  title = "Coral Survival Curves"
)

# Cox model evaluating Treatment, Phenotype, and their interaction
cox_model <- coxph(surv_object ~ Treatment * Phenotype * Date, data = surv_cleaned)

# View the summary results (look for p-values and Exp(coef) which is the Hazard Ratio)
summary(cox_model)
```
